words.def: pleased; happy
covering or affecting the whole world
words.example: This year the global economy will grow by about 4 per cent.

words.def: a piece of clothing for the hand, made of wool, leather, etc. with separate parts for each finger and the thumb
a person’s turn to move or play in a game or an activity
things that are produced to be sold
a person who has a university degree
feeling or showing thanks because somebody has done something kind for you or has done as you asked
words.example: Thank you for doing this. I really am so grateful.

words.def: the process in people, animals or plants of growing physically, mentally or emotionally
words.example: Lack of water will stunt the plant's growth.

words.def: a person, such as a soldier, a police officer or a prison officer, who protects a place or people, or prevents prisoners from escaping
an attempt to give an answer or an opinion when you cannot be certain if you are right
a person who shows other people the way to a place, especially somebody employed to show tourists around interesting places
words.example: Our tour guide showed us around the old town.

words.def: feeling ashamed because you have done something that you know is wrong or have not done something that you should have done
words.example: Matt and Chrissy both looked equally guilty.

words.def: a room or hall with equipment for doing physical exercise, for example in a school
words.example: The school has recently built a new gym.

words.def: to the extent of half
the part of the body at the end of the arm, including the fingers and thumb
words.example: Ian placed a hand on her shoulder.

words.def: the state of feeling or showing pleasure
almost no; almost not; almost none
a very strong feeling of dislike for somebody
words.example: She gave him a look of real hate.

words.def: the part of the body on top of the neck containing the eyes, nose, mouth and brain
words.example: She nodded her head in agreement.

words.def: the title of a news article printed in large letters, especially at the top of the front page on a newspaper or the home page on a news website
the process of supplying heat to a room or building; a system used to do this
to a great degree; in large amounts
the measurement of how tall a person or thing is
words.example: Height: 210 mm. Width: 57 mm. Length: 170 mm.

words.def: very
words.example: It is highly unlikely that she'll be late.

words.def: important in history; likely to be thought of as important at some time in the future
connected with the past
words.example: You must place these events in their historical context.

words.def: an act of hitting somebody/something with your hand or with an object held in your hand
always telling the truth, and never stealing or cheating
very bad or unpleasant; used to describe something that you do not like
a feeling of great shock or fear
words.example: The thought of being left alone filled her with horror.

words.def: extremely large in size or amount; great in degree
words.example: The sums of money involved are potentially huge.

words.def: a violent storm with very strong winds, especially in the western Atlantic Ocean
words.example: A powerful hurricane hit the Florida coast.

words.def: the need or wish to get something done quickly
who or what somebody/something is
words.example: The police are trying to discover the identity of the killer.

words.def: to pay no attention to something
words.example: He ignored all the ‘No Smoking’ signs and lit up a cigarette.

words.def: not allowed by the law
the state of being physically or mentally ill
words.example: The virus can cause illness in humans.

words.def: existing only in your mind or imagination
words.example: The equator is an imaginary line around the middle of the earth.

words.def: happening or done without delay
without delay
the powerful effect that something has on somebody/something
the quality of being important
words.example: She stressed the importance of careful preparation.

words.def: an idea, a feeling or an opinion that you get about somebody/something, or that somebody/something gives you
words.example: My first impression of him was favourable.

words.def: making you admire them, because they are very large, good, skilful, etc.
the act of making something better; the process of something becoming better
words.example: The economy has shown significant improvement over the past 9 months.

words.def: having something as part of a group or set
words.example: I've got three days' holiday including New Year's Day.

words.def: a rise in the amount, number or value of something
impossible or very difficult to believe
extremely
used after very and an adjective or adverb to emphasize a statement, description, etc.
having its own government
to show that something is true or exists
words.example: indicate something, Initial tests indicate the presence of oxygen.

words.def: happening not as the main aim, cause or result of a particular action, but in addition to it
words.example: the indirect effects of the war

words.def: considered separately rather than as part of a group
words.example: We interviewed each individual member of the community.
